heroHeadline: Iran FM Flies to Pakistan for Peace Talks; Brent Hits $107; Michigan Sentiment Confirms All-Time Record Low; FOMC Now 4 Days Away
heroSubtitle: Apr 24: Iranian FM Araghchi flew to Islamabad for US-Iran indirect peace talks via Pakistani mediators — the first diplomatic movement since IRGC ship seizures in the Strait of Hormuz on April 23. Oil surged sharply: WTI rose 1.59% to $97.37/barrel, Brent 1.91% to $107.10 — posting ~18% weekly gains as the US naval blockade remains in effect. The University of Michigan's final April 2026 Consumer Sentiment confirmed all-time record lows (~47.6); year-ahead inflation expectations held at 4.8%. Durable goods orders for March came in flat (0.0%, missed 1.1% forecast). S&P 500 rebounded +0.3% (Nasdaq +0.8%) on diplomacy optimism and Intel earnings. Kevin Warsh Fed Chair confirmation remains blocked by Sen. Tillis with Powell's term expiring May 15 (21 days). FOMC April 28-29 and Q1 GDP advance (April 30) are the twin central risk events.
footerNote: Data sourced from IMF WEO Apr 2026, IEA Oil Market Report Apr 2026, University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers, US Census Bureau, S&P Global Flash PMI Apr 2026, BIS, Federal Reserve FOMC, ECB Governing Council, Bank of England, BEA, BLS, World Bank, Tax Foundation, Penn Wharton Budget Model, Conference Board, CBOE, Goldman Sachs Research, JPMorgan Global Markets Strategy, Moody's Analytics, EIA, China NBS, CBP CAPE Portal, CNN Business, Bloomberg, CNBC, The National News, Al Jazeera, NPR, NBC News, 24/7 Wall St.
